---
title: "Software"
toc: false
---

Research software for computational conflict analysis. Everything is open
source; bug reports and contributions are welcome on GitHub.

::: {.software-feature}
::: {.software-hex}
[![](images/conflibertr-hex.png){fig-alt="conflibertR hex logo"}](software/conflibertr.qmd)
:::
::: {.software-body}
[conflibertR]{.software-name}

An R package bringing [ConfliBERT](https://eventdata.utdallas.edu/), the
NSF-funded language model for political conflict and violence, to the R
ecosystem: entity recognition, event classification, question answering,
fine-tuning, model comparison, and active learning, all from R. On CRAN
as of July 2026.

[[Package page](software/conflibertr.qmd) [CRAN](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=conflibertR) [GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/conflibertR) [Cheatsheet (PDF)](software/conflibertR-cheatsheet.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[ConfliBERT GUI]{.pub-title}
[Browser-based NLP toolkit for conflict and political violence text analysis: NER, classification, question answering, and LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning.]{.pub-meta}
[[GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/ConfliBERT-gui)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Confli-mBERT]{.pub-title}
[ModernBERT fine-tuned on the Global Terrorism Database for conflict event classification; companion model to the paper on NLP model selection in computational social science.]{.pub-meta}
[[Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/shreyasmeher/Confli-mBERT)]{.pub-links}
:::
